title: Internal Audit Record
description: Signed, content-addressed, RFC 8785-canonicalized record of a non-customer-touching agent action evaluated against a Scope Contract. Issued by agents whose direct actions do not produce Compliance Receipts (LeadScraper, Validator, InboxAllocator, Personalizer, COO, InboxProvisioner, RegulatoryMonitor — see agents.md §1) and by customer-touching agents (CampaignManager, ResponseHandler, Voice) for their non-customer-touching operations (Loop adjustments, daily reports, internal corrections). Shares the signing/chaining crypto substrate with Compliance Receipts; discriminated by record_type. License: MIT (SPDX-License-Identifier: MIT). Part of AgentMarshal — github.com/ApexForge13/agentmarshal.

internal_audit_version.description: Internal Audit Record artifact version. Independent of schema_version so a v0.1 record format MAY be re-validated against a v0.2 schema if backward-compatible.

schema_version.description: Version of this JSON Schema document the record conforms to. Verifiers MAY select a schema by this field when running against a multi-version verifier.

record_type.description: Discriminator that distinguishes this envelope from Compliance Receipts. Verifiers dispatch on this field. Compliance Receipts produced under v0.1 do not carry record_type (implicit 'compliance_receipt'); a verifier seeing no record_type MUST treat the record as a Compliance Receipt and dispatch to the receipt verifier.

record_id.description: Globally unique identifier for this audit record. Format: 'ia-' prefix followed by a UUIDv4. The prefix lets operators visually disambiguate audit record IDs from receipt IDs in logs and traces.

audit_hash.description: SHA-256 hex of the canonicalized audit record MINUS this field itself. Computed AFTER signatures are appended, over the union of the body and the signatures array. Provides content-addressing and an integrity check independent of signatures.

previous_audit_hash.description: audit_hash of the prior audit record in this agent's audit-record hash chain, or null when this is the first audit record for the agent. Per-agent per-record-type chains: receipts and audit records form parallel chains, so an agent emitting both has independent previous_receipt_hash and previous_audit_hash sequences.

canonical_form.description: Algorithm used to produce the canonical byte sequence that is signed and hashed. Declared in-band so future canonicalization rotations do not invalidate old records.

issued_at.description: ISO 8601 UTC timestamp at which the audit record was constructed (after evaluation; before signing).

code_version.description: Identifier of the AgentMarshal codebase version that produced this record. RECOMMENDED: 40-char git SHA-1. Falls back to AGENTMARSHAL_CODE_VERSION env var or the literal 'unknown' when git is unavailable.

tenant_id.description: Operator tenant namespace, echoed from the contract.

evaluation_id.description: Identifier of the underlying evaluation audit row. Joins the audit record to the full AuthZEN-shaped request/response that produced it (when the action was routed through evaluate()).

request_id.description: Correlation identifier from the call that triggered evaluation. For internal actions without a PEP-originated request, producers SHOULD synthesize a stable internal ID (e.g., 'internal-<uuid>') so trace correlation still works.

agent.description: Identity of the agent emitting this audit record. The enum on 'type' constrains records to the canonical 10-agent inventory from agents.md §1; any of the 10 may emit internal-audit records for non-customer-touching actions.

id.description: Per-instance agent identifier. Unique within tenant.

type.description: Agent class from the canonical inventory (agents.md §1).

version.description: Agent code version. String to allow semver, build tags, or operator-defined labels.

action.description: The internal action being recorded. action.type is a free-string identifier in v0.2 (registry comes with a later bubble when agents are wired); inputs and outputs are open objects so each agent can capture action-specific provenance.

type.description: Identifier for the kind of action (e.g., 'template_promoted', 'inbox_retired', 'pipeline_buffer_adjusted'). Free-string in v0.2; an enum registry will replace this in a later schema version.

inputs.description: Action inputs that fed the agent's decision. Schema permits arbitrary keys; agents are responsible for not embedding secrets.

outputs.description: Action outputs produced by the agent. Schema permits arbitrary keys.

contract.description: Scope Contract under which the action was evaluated. Echoes the contract's identity at evaluation time.

version.description: Version label of the contract that was active at evaluation time. String to allow semver, supersession sequences, or operator-defined version labels.

evaluation.description: Evaluation trace for this action. Same composite_evaluations / decision / predicate_evaluations shape as Compliance Receipts, nested under 'evaluation' so the receipt vs. audit-record discriminator stays clean at the top level.

predicate_evaluations.description: Ordered trace of per-rule predicate evaluations performed during this evaluation.

composite_evaluations.description: Ordered trace of composite predicate evaluations (TCPA, CAN-SPAM, sourcing, operational, voice). Composite results other than 'pass' block 'allow' decisions via the fail-safe policy.

effect.description: Decision applied to the request. 'allow' permits the action; 'deny' blocks it; 'escalate' defers to a human or higher-authority workflow.

evaluation_path.description: Phase of evaluation that produced the decision.

matched_rule_id.description: rule_id of the declared_scope rule that matched. Non-null only when evaluation_path is 'declared_scope'.

review_required.description: OPTIONAL (Bubble 16 three-state). True when the decision blocks the action pending human review (some composite returned 'review' and none returned 'fail') rather than denying outright. Absent ⇒ false; backward-compatible with all pre-Bubble-16 records, which omit it. evaluation.decision.effect remains the strict allow/deny/escalate vocabulary; this envelope field carries the richer status.

review_reason.description: OPTIONAL (Bubble 16). Human-readable reason for the review hold; present only when review_required is true.

bd_calls.description: OPTIONAL (Bubble 17). Ordered audit entries for governed Bright Data tool calls made during this evaluation via the AgentMarshal MCP proxy. Absent ⇒ no BD calls (backward-compatible; pre-Bubble-17 records omit it). Part of the signed body, so tampering with any bd_call breaks the signature.

regulatory_state.description: Anchor that ties this audit record to a point-in-time snapshot of external regulatory data. Same shape as Compliance Receipts.

hash.description: SHA-256 hex of the canonicalized regulatory snapshot. Null when pending.

pending.description: True when no regulatory snapshot was available at evaluation time. Verifiers MAY downgrade trust accordingly.

snapshot_source.description: Identifier of the data source the snapshot was derived from. Null when pending.

anchor_timestamp.description: ISO 8601 UTC timestamp at which the snapshot was anchored to an external timestamping service. Null when pending or when no external anchor was used.

anchor_method.description: External anchoring method used. 'pending' is the v0.1 default until Bright Data integration wires real anchors.

signatures.description: One or more signatures over the canonicalized body (the record MINUS audit_hash, signatures, and timestamp_token). Same shape as Compliance Receipts; v0.1 uses Ed25519 with the same KeyRegistry as receipts.

timestamp_token.description: OPTIONAL RFC 3161 external timestamp anchor over audit_hash. Same shape and semantics as Compliance Receipts' timestamp_token: attached after signing, excluded from audit_hash and the signed body, stripped before verification. null or absent ⇒ not externally timestamped.

PredicateEvaluation.description: Per-predicate evaluation record echoed from the AuthZEN evaluator. Identical shape to Compliance Receipt PredicateEvaluation $def.

rule_id.description: rule_id within which this predicate was evaluated. Null when evaluation_path is 'out_of_scope' or 'temporal'.

predicate_path.description: Dot-path to the predicate within the match block.

constraint.description: Verbatim copy of the predicate value from the contract.

actual_value.description: The value from the request at predicate_path. May be null when the property is absent.

result.description: Whether this predicate passed, failed, or was skipped.

reason.description: Optional human-readable reason for fail/skipped results.

CompositePredicateEvaluation.description: Per-composite predicate evaluation entry (TCPA, CAN-SPAM, sourcing, operational, voice). Identical shape to Compliance Receipt CompositePredicateEvaluation $def.

result.description: Composite result. 'pass' is allowable; 'fail', 'stub', and 'review' block allow under the fail-safe policy. 'review' (Bubble 16) marks a possible-match that blocks pending human review, distinct from a hard 'fail'.

reason.description: Human-readable reason produced by the composite predicate.

details.description: Open trace bag from the composite predicate.

BDCallAudit.description: Audit record of one governed Bright Data call captured into the record. Identical shape to the Compliance Receipt BDCallAudit $def. Execution fields are null when the call was denied (never executed) or failed before a response was captured.

service.description: Bright Data service the call targeted.

matched_rule_id.description: rule_id of the bd_permissions rule that governed the call; null when no rule matched (deny).

governance_result.description: MCP-proxy governance decision for the call.

composite_outcomes.description: Outcomes of the bd_permissions rule's composite checks, in order.

executed_at.description: ISO 8601 UTC time the BD call was executed; null when denied/not executed.

duration_ms.description: BD call wall-clock duration in ms; null when not executed.

response_sha256.description: SHA-256 hex of the raw BD response body (fingerprint); null when no response captured.

response_size_bytes.description: Byte length of the raw BD response; null when no response captured.

bd_request_id.description: Bright Data's own request id for cross-referencing; null when unavailable.

BDCompositeOutcome.description: Outcome of one composite check run during BD-call governance.

result.description: Composite result. 'pass' is allowable; 'fail', 'stub', and 'review' block the rule.

AuditRecordSignature.description: One signature over the canonical body. Same shape as Compliance Receipt's ReceiptSignature; same KeyRegistry, same algorithm set.

algorithm.description: Signature algorithm. v0.1 supports Ed25519 only.

key_id.description: RFC 7638 JWK thumbprint of the public key used, prefixed with 'am-'.

public_key_fingerprint.description: SHA-256 hex of the raw 32-byte Ed25519 public key.

signature.description: Hex-encoded signature bytes. Ed25519 = 128 hex chars.

signed_at.description: ISO 8601 UTC timestamp at which this signature was produced.

signer_role.description: Role of the signing party. 'agentmarshal' is the substrate; 'operator' is the agent's principal; 'vendor' is a downstream system.

TimestampToken.description: An RFC 3161 external timestamp token anchoring audit_hash to a third-party Time Stamping Authority. Same shape as Compliance Receipts' TimestampToken $def. v0.2 uses FreeTSA.

token_b64.description: base64 of the DER-encoded RFC 3161 TimeStampToken (a CMS SignedData ContentInfo).

issued_at.description: ISO 8601 UTC genTime extracted from the token's TSTInfo at issuance. Informational; verifiers re-derive the authoritative time from token_b64.
